cases.notes: Vendored Noto Naskh Arabic changes the visual-order glyph sequence for سلام beyond pure RTL reordering of raw cmap glyph IDs, giving bounded joining-form evidence without promoting full Arabic support.
Vendored Noto Naskh Arabic proves a bounded base-plus-mark case by tying the positioning or zero-advance check to the mark cluster itself.

Reviewed repo fixture gpos-missing-gdef.otf keeps Arabic base-plus-mark input on the stable generic gdef-required refusal path instead of approximating mark attachment without glyph classification data.
Single-run shaping emits the paragraph-owned bidi diagnostic instead of silently approximating mixed Arabic/LTR layout, while still returning shaped output.

remainingGates.detail: Vendored Noto Naskh Arabic still has only a bounded runtime check for logical text \u0644\u0627 changing away from the raw visual-order cmap pair; this wave does not yet prove a feature-local lam-alef positive row.
Positive cursive-attachment evidence is still proven only on the bounded reviewed M6-005 fixture family, not yet on the vendored Noto Arabic ticket-local fixture wave.

Dedicated arabic-missing-cursive/arabic-missing-mark fixtures and narrower text.shaping.arabic-* refusal codes remain absent; the reviewed generic gdef-required row is bounded evidence only and does not replace those Arabic-specific refusal assets.
